
Two arXiv papers from the RSS feed meet ≥3 inclusion criteria with strong signal: HyEvo addresses self-evolving hybrid agentic workflow architecture with iterative refinement, and the utility-guided orchestration paper directly targets tool-use and agent orchestration design. Grok social signals were largely unverifiable or pointed to resources already covered in spirit by existing README entries.

## Suggestions

- [HyEvo: Self-Evolving Hybrid Agentic Workflows for Efficient Reasoning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19639) — Proposes a self-evolving hybrid agentic workflow architecture that dynamically adapts workflow structure for efficient reasoning, with empirical evaluation across benchmarks.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Iterative refinement practices (evaluation loops, human-in-the-loop, observability), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)

- [Utility-Guided Agent Orchestration for Efficient LLM Tool Use](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19896) — Introduces a utility-guided orchestration framework for selecting and sequencing LLM tool use efficiently, with empirical benchmarking across agentic task settings.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)
